Release","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":""},{"id":264274051,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNjQyNzQwNTE=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/component:%20distribution","name":"component: distribution","color":"009800","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":27,"created_at":"2022-11-04T12:52:17Z","updated_at":"2024-10-03T13:12:36Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Proposed new feature or change:

Currently there are no Windows ARM64 wheels (ending with `-win_arm64`) provided for NumPy on PyPI. [cibuildwheel](https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel) started supporting cross-compiling Windows ARM64 wheels with their [2.11 release](https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/releases/tag/v2.11.0), which is [currently used](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/22524) in NumPy.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/22530/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/22530/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"reopened"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2391389955","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/22530#issuecomment-2391389955","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/22530","id":2391389955,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86OibcD","user":{"login":"Molier","id":7408635,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc0MDg2MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7408635?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier","html_url":"https://github.com/Molier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-03T13:12:35Z","updated_at":"2024-10-03T13:12:35Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hi! I am working on an arm64 windows device as well (surface 11 / copilot+ pc) and would like to help testing and anything that could be useful to speed up the process of having these work better on arm64. I have tried to build them manually using a combo of ninja, llvm 19.1, cmake etc... 

> FWIW, I am using Python for Windows on ARM64 on a [Windows Dev Kit 2003](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/arm/dev-kit/). It's working well for me. For example, latest numpy and scipy pass all tests with OpenBLAS. I shared my builds at https://github.com/cgohlke/win_arm64-wheels/.

But I could not get it to work for the time being, biggest issue being the fortran compiler, but even with that installed from llvm, i have issues with meson and some errors `clang-19: error: unsupported option '-mlong-double-64' for target 'aarch64-w64-windows-gnu'` .

Could you also maybe shine some light on your specific software setup @cgohlke ? Would be a big help, I`m trying to develop as natively as possible. ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2391389955/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Comment:

Hello,

we would like to contribute to conda-forge by helping to enable Windows arm64 architecture.

From what I understood, miniconda installer is developed by Anaconda, and it's code is not opened (to the opposite of miniforge).
Is that possible to add support for an "alien" python (arm64) in conda, or does Anaconda only can do this (before even talking about building packages)?

Thanks,
Pierrick","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/miniforge/issues/297/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/miniforge/issues/297/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/miniforge/issues/comments/2398925126","html_url":"https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/issues/297#issuecomment-2398925126","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/miniforge/issues/297","id":2398925126,"node_id":"IC_kwDODTUbkM6O_LFG","user":{"login":"Molier","id":7408635,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc0MDg2MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7408635?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier","html_url":"https://github.com/Molier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Molier/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-08T06:04:20Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T06:04:20Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"any update on conda for windows arm64 or WoA ?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/miniforge/issues/comments/2398925126/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
